s1_executive_summary.delivery_focus: This quarter the delivery team focused on completing the NovaPay merchant onboarding module, stabilising the payment gateway integration, and launching the first version of the analytics dashboard. On the engineering side, this quarter engineering focused on delivering the merchant onboarding APIs, completing Stripe payment gateway integration, building the analytics data pipeline using event sourcing, and upgrading the CI/CD pipeline to support zero-downtime deployments.
s1_executive_summary.highlights: merchant onboarding module delivered; payment gateway integration with Stripe and local PSPs completed
s1_executive_summary.areas_requiring_attention: backend team capacity; local PSP integration still pending final vendor certification
s1_executive_summary.next_quarter_preview: Q3 must focus on three things. First, production launch readiness — hardening deployment pipelines, completing load testing at 3x expected traffic, and resolving all open medium-severity findings.

s2_service_overview.active_services: Active services include SaaS product delivery, QA, DevOps, and product consulting.
s2_service_overview.delivery_model: Delivery model is a dedicated team operating on two-week sprints.
s2_service_overview.key_stakeholders: Key customer stakeholders are the CTO and Head of Product at NovaPay; internal stakeholders are the Delivery Manager and Account Manager at SELISE.
s2_service_overview.team_composition: Team composition includes 3 backend engineers, 2 frontend engineers, 1 QA engineer, 1 DevOps engineer, and 1 BA.
s2_service_overview.reporting_cadence: Reporting cadence is bi-weekly syncs with the customer and a monthly steering call.

s3_achievements.impact: reduces customer onboarding time from 3 days to under 4 hours, directly enabling NovaPay to scale merchant acquisition

s3_achievements.achievement: Payment gateway integration with Stripe and local PSPs completed
s3_achievements.impact: unblocks live transaction processing for the first 50 pilot merchants
gives NovaPay's ops team real-time visibility into transaction volumes, failure rates, and merchant activity

s3_achievements.achievement: Security audit passed with no critical findings
s3_achievements.impact: strengthens customer confidence ahead of the planned public launch in Q3

s3_achievements.achievement: Merchant onboarding APIs built and deployed
s3_achievements.impact: all endpoints documented, test coverage at 87%

s3_achievements.achievement: Stripe integration completed with webhook support for real-time

s4_delivery_summary.summary: delivered on time, UAT signed off by customer, live in staging
Stripe integration complete, local PSP integration delayed by 3 weeks due to API documentation issues on vendor side, now resolved
launched with core metrics, additional KPIs deferred to Q3
CI/CD pipeline upgraded but production deployment faced two rollback incidents during the quarter
audit completed, all medium findings remediated

s5_metrics.comment: Average ticket response time: target 4 hours, actual 3.2 hours.
Release success rate: target 90%, actual 85% due to two rollback incidents.
Defect leakage to production: target below 5%, actual 7%.

major_incidents.issue: production deployment failure on release v2.4.1
major_incidents.impact: 45 minutes of staging environment downtime affecting UAT session
major_incidents.root_cause: misconfigured environment variable in deployment script

major_incidents.issue: analytics dashboard returning stale data for 2 hours
major_incidents.impact: incorrect metrics shown to customer during demo
major_incidents.root_cause: Redis cache invalidation bug introduced in v2.5.0

s7_quality_health.observation: Code quality is good overall — peer review process maintained, average PR review time is 18 hours.
s7_quality_health.improvement_action: Maintain current code quality practices into next quarter.

s7_quality_health.observation: QA has been thorough but slightly under-resourced with one QA engineer covering the full scope, leading to some test cases deferred.
s7_quality_health.improvement_action: Define a corrective action plan for qa ahead of next quarter.

s7_quality_health.observation: Release management improved with blue-green deployment but the two rollback incidents highlighted gaps in pre-release environment variable validation.
s7_quality_health.improvement_action: Define a corrective action plan for release management ahead of next quarter.

s7_quality_health.observation: Documentation is adequate for APIs but internal architecture docs are behind — this is tech debt we need to address in Q3.

s7_quality_health.improvement_action: Maintain current communication practices into next quarter.

s7_quality_health.observation: Team stability is good — no attrition, though the backend team is operating at near full capacity with limited buffer for unplanned work.
s7_quality_health.improvement_action: Define a corrective action plan for team stability ahead of next quarter.

s8_risks.mitigation: to evaluate adding one backend engineer for Q3 before public launch peak

s8_risks.description: Local PSP integration still pending final vendor certification
s8_risks.mitigation: weekly follow-up with vendor and parallel preparation of fallback flow
to allocate 2 hours per week in Q3 for documentation catch-up

s8_risks.description: NovaPay infrastructure team needs to provision production environment by July 15 for Q3 launch readiness

s9_customer_feedback.satisfaction: Customer satisfaction is moderate this quarter.
s9_customer_feedback.communication: Communication has been consistent with bi-weekly calls maintained throughout.
s9_customer_feedback.responsiveness: Responsiveness from SELISE side has been strong — no missed SLAs on communication.
s9_customer_feedback.business_alignment: Business alignment is good overall, with the customer confirming Q3 priorities around public launch readiness.
s9_customer_feedback.areas_of_concern: NovaPay's CTO expressed appreciation for the onboarding module delivery but raised concerns about the PSP integration delay and the production rollback incidents.

s10_value_delivered.business_value: Delivery of Merchant onboarding module delivered, Payment gateway integration with Stripe and local PSPs completed translated into measurable customer-facing gains, against a backdrop of 3.7/5.0 CSAT versus a 4.0/5.0 target.
s10_value_delivered.operational_value: 34 of 38 support tickets were resolved with 0 critical incidents, though 2 major incident(s) this quarter point to remaining gaps in release validation.
s10_value_delivered.technical_value: Engineering shipped 6 notable technical achievements this quarter, while 4 of 6 quality dimensions still need attention before the next release cycle.
s10_value_delivered.strategic_value: The current trajectory positions the team for its next milestone, contingent on resolving 4 open risk/issue/dependency item(s) tracked this quarter.

s11_lessons_learned.lesson: Incident on June 3 2026 exposed a gap in misconfigured environment variable in deployment script.
s11_lessons_learned.context: Impact: 45 minutes of staging environment downtime affecting UAT session. Resolved via: immediate rollback and config fix.
s11_lessons_learned.action: Add an automated pre-release check that would have caught this class of failure.

s11_lessons_learned.lesson: Incident on June 18 2026 exposed a gap in Redis cache invalidation bug introduced in v2.5.0.
s11_lessons_learned.context: Impact: incorrect metrics shown to customer during demo. Resolved via: hotfix deployed within 3 hours.
s11_lessons_learned.action: Add an automated pre-release check that would have caught this class of failure.

s11_lessons_learned.lesson: QA is trending Amber and needs structural attention, not just a one-off fix.

s11_lessons_learned.action: Define a corrective action plan for qa ahead of next quarter.

s11_lessons_learned.context: Release management improved with blue-green deployment but the two rollback incidents highlighted gaps in pre-release environment variable validation.
s11_lessons_learned.action: Define a corrective action plan for release management ahead of next quarter.

Heading into next quarter, the priority is: production launch readiness — hardening deployment pipelines, completing...; local PSP integration completion and certification — this...; addressing tech debt: architecture documentation, test coverage improvement....
